slash_commands.name: init
slash_commands.description: Analyze the codebase and generate an `AGENTS.md` file

slash_commands.name: compact
slash_commands.description: Compact the context

slash_commands.name: clear
slash_commands.description: Clear the context

slash_commands.name: yolo
slash_commands.description: Toggle YOLO mode (auto-approve all actions)

slash_commands.name: add-dir
slash_commands.description: Add a directory to the workspace. Usage: /add-dir <path>. Run without args to list added dirs

slash_commands.name: skill:Check
slash_commands.description:
Final validation before implementation begins. The Checker Agent reviews plans for 
completeness, identifies risks, and ensures nothing critical is missed. Invoke when 
user says "check", "validate", "?prep", or when transitioning from planning to implementation.

slash_commands.name: skill:Kimi
slash_commands.description:
Contents: Complete Kimi CLI architecture, agents, skills, tools, configuration, troubleshooting.
Reason: Answer any question about how Kimi CLI works or how to use it.
Intention: User asks about Kimi CLI mechanics, wants to create agents/skills, or says "how do you work?"

slash_commands.name: skill:Plan
slash_commands.description:
Structured planning workflow with clarifying questions, plan output to pipeline/launch/, 
and gotcha checking. Invoke when user wraps content in <plan>...</plan> tags, types 
"/plan", or says "let's plan this" before starting work.

slash_commands.name: skill:approval
slash_commands.description:
Final stage completion agent. Merges validated work into main codebase, updates 
all documentation (DECISIONS.md, AGENTS.md, README.md, skills), audits spec compliance, 
generates completion reports, and moves jobs to pipeline/complete/. Trigger: 
user says "/approval", "approve this", "finalize", or when build validation passes.

slash_commands.name: skill:architecture
slash_commands.description:
Layered architecture validator for Universal Design Spec compliance. Validates 
View → Event Bus → Controller → Service → State patterns, dependency direction, 
naming conventions, and file organization. Trigger: user says "check architecture", 
"does this follow spec?", "how to structure?", or during code review of organization.

slash_commands.name: skill:audit
slash_commands.description:
Documentation consistency auditor and improvement opportunity finder. Cross-references 
AGENTS.md, README.md, spec/, DECISIONS.md, skills/ against reality to find discrepancies, 
stale info, missing updates. Proposes new skills, doc tweaks, process improvements. 
Trigger: user says "audit", "check docs", "what's stale?", or seeking improvement opportunities.

slash_commands.name: skill:build
slash_commands.description:
Plan execution agent with embedded validation checkpoints. Reads orchestrated plans 
from pipeline/build/, executes steps sequentially, runs validation scripts at checkpoints, 
references indicated skills, stops on failure. Trigger: user says "/build", "execute plan", 
"start building", or when preflight completes.

slash_commands.name: skill:bulletin
slash_commands.description:
PHOENIX BULLETIN BOARD SYSTEM - Task queue for AI agent coordination.
PRIMARY system for tracking work between RC, Phoenix, and specialist agents.
Use when: creating tasks, moving tasks, checking task status, workflow coordination.
NOT for email - use 'email' skill for AgentMail.
NOT for documentation - use 'wiki' skill for wiki updates.

slash_commands.name: skill:components
slash_commands.description:
Portable JavaScript component creator. Generates self-contained UI components (toast, 
modal, drawer, confirmation, context-menu) that create own DOM, inject own styles, 
clean up after themselves. Works across projects without rewiring. Trigger: user says 
"create component", "build modal/toast/drawer", or implementing dynamic UI elements.

slash_commands.name: skill:css-layout-debug
slash_commands.description: No description provided.

slash_commands.name: skill:design
slash_commands.description:
Universal Design Spec compliance checker and architectural guidance provider. Quick 
heuristics for spec adherence, deep reference to spec modules when needed. Validates 
architecture decisions, component patterns, token usage. Trigger: user says "check spec", 
"does this follow design?", "how should I structure?", or during system design.

slash_commands.name: skill:email
slash_commands.description:
PHOENIX EMAIL SYSTEM (AgentMail) - For quick communication and notifications.
Use when: sending quick messages, checking inbox, email notifications.
NOT for task tracking - use 'bulletin' skill for tasks.

slash_commands.name: skill:github-cli
slash_commands.description: No description provided.

slash_commands.name: skill:guidance
slash_commands.description:
Active code quality guardian for HTML/CSS/JS. Pre-flight context scanning, convention 
enforcement, security validation (XSS, path safety, export safety). Catches issues 
before they happen. Trigger: user says "guidance", "check my work", "review this", 
or during web development planning.

slash_commands.name: skill:kimi-cli-help
slash_commands.description: Answer Kimi Code CLI usage, configuration, and troubleshooting questions. Use when user asks about Kimi Code CLI installation, setup, configuration, slash commands, keyboard shortcuts, MCP integration, providers, environment variables, how something works internally, or any questions about Kimi Code CLI itself.

slash_commands.name: skill:kimi-details
slash_commands.description: Quick reference for Kimi CLI operation, context layers, session management, and Command Sync integration. Use when user asks "How do you work?", "What files do you read?", "How does context work?", "Explain Kimi CLI", or needs to understand the 5-layer context system, session management, or project setup patterns.

slash_commands.name: skill:launch
slash_commands.description:
Pipeline entry point agent. Transforms conversation sessions into structured job 
specifications (spec.md), creates job folders in pipeline/preflight/, initializes 
build context. First stage of 3-bot pipeline. Trigger: user says "/launch", 
"start pipeline", "create spec", or beginning new project/feature.

slash_commands.name: skill:phoenix
slash_commands.description:
Phoenix AI agent workflow and GitLab sync management. Handles the handoff 
between RC (GitHub) and Phoenix (GitLab) for sandboxed AI development work.
Use when user mentions "phoenix", "gitlab sync", "ai agent", or "sandbox workflow".

slash_commands.name: skill:pipeline
slash_commands.description:
Six-stage tunable workflow orchestrator. Coordinates launch → preflight → build → 
approval → complete stages across multiple Kimi CLI instances. Each stage independently 
optimizable, observable, automatable. Trigger: user says "/pipeline", "/yolo", 
"start workflow", or managing multi-stage jobs.

slash_commands.name: skill:preflight
slash_commands.description:
Planning and validation checkpoint agent. Reads jobs from pipeline/preflight/, 
gathers context, creates structured plans with embedded validation scripts, assigns 
skill dependencies, orchestrates build instructions. Trigger: user says "/preflight", 
"plan this", "check before building", or when launch completes.

slash_commands.name: skill:refactor
slash_commands.description:
Code organization and refactoring guidance agent. File size analysis, dependency 
validation, code organization rules, alignment with Universal Design Spec. Determines 
when and how to refactor. Trigger: user says "should I refactor?", "is this file too big?", 
"check dependencies", "bring into alignment", or reviewing code organization.

slash_commands.name: skill:resume
slash_commands.description:
Conversation recovery and episodic memory retrieval agent. Locates recent sessions 
from ~/.kimi/sessions/, summarizes context, supports agent-type-scoped memory queries. 
Trigger: user says "resume", "what were we doing?", "recover session", or agent 
memory queries.

slash_commands.name: skill:scratchpad
slash_commands.description:
Non-actionable idea capture agent. Temporary holding area for thoughts, questions, 
research notes, feature ideas that don't exist yet. Items graduate to TODO (actionable) 
or DECISIONS (patterns) or deleted (stale). Trigger: user says "add to scratchpad", 
"note this idea", or capturing non-actionable thoughts.

slash_commands.name: skill:skill-creator
slash_commands.description: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Kimi's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.

slash_commands.name: skill:structure
slash_commands.description:
Project file structure and folder organization agent. Universal Design Spec compliance 
for scaffolding new projects. Dependency rules, file placement guidelines. Trigger: 
user says "set up project structure", "how to organize files?", "where does this go?", 
or scaffolding new projects.

slash_commands.name: skill:sync
slash_commands.description:
Session management and conversation persistence agent. Handles saving, loading, 
organizing chat sessions for context recovery and episodic memory. Trigger: user 
says "/sync-chat", "sync the chat", "save session", or managing conversation history.

slash_commands.name: skill:todo
slash_commands.description:
Actionable task tracking agent. Work that must be done, will break system or block 
progress if not completed. Auto-adds from TODO/FIXME/XXX markers in code. Trigger: 
user says "add to todo", "this needs to be done", or discovering incomplete implementations.

slash_commands.name: skill:tokens
slash_commands.description:
CSS design tokens and theme system agent. Establishes single source of truth for 
colors, spacing, typography, shadows. Token-based styling for consistent, portable 
theming across projects. Trigger: user says "set up design tokens", "define CSS variables", 
"theme setup", or creating/implementing design systems.

slash_commands.name: skill:update
slash_commands.description:
File freshness tracking and staleness detection agent. Monitors skill versions, 
spec alignment, documentation currency. Identifies what needs updating. Trigger: 
user says "what's stale?", "check for updates", "what needs refreshing?", or 
maintaining documentation/skill currency.

slash_commands.name: skill:validate
slash_commands.description:
Retrospective validation and gap detection agent. Validates against plans, requirements, 
conversation history to catch overlooked features before considering work complete. 
"Did we miss anything?" safety net. Trigger: user says "validate", "did we miss anything?", 
"check my work", or expressing concern something is missing.

slash_commands.name: skill:whisper
slash_commands.description:
Contents: Server-Sent Events (SSE) "whisper line" pattern for backend-to-frontend real-time updates.
Reason: Implement efficient real-time communication without polling or WebSockets.
Intention: User says "set up SSE", "backend push", "real-time updates", "whisper line", or when implementing live data features.

slash_commands.name: skill:wiki
slash_commands.description:
PHOENIX WIKI SYSTEM - Central knowledge base for agent onboarding.
CRITICAL RULE: Every wiki update MUST have a corresponding bulletin board task.
Read the Wiki Update Process wiki page BEFORE making any edits.

payload.user_input: Read the file scripts/capture-wire-output.js and tell me what it does in one sentence.

return_value.message: 89 lines read from file starting from line 1. End of file reached.
